name: Source Fidelity review for Acceptable Use Operational Training Augmentation Update bundle
summary: Accepted. The update reuses four already published full-text sources and does not introduce new extraction claims. The staged claim keeps the New Mexico evidence limited to documented training augmentation, keeps Rezayi and Djagba tied to real-response evaluation boundaries, and keeps Wang as adjacent constructed off-topic detection evidence.

findings.claim_or_issue: The New Mexico technical report supports operational training augmentation after review and approval, but does not provide enough extracted detail for synthetic-only validation, subgroup fairness, cut-score, or deployment-readiness claims.
findings.why_it_matters: Acceptable-use language should not overstate what one technical report demonstrates.
findings.recommended_action: Keep the staged summary and limitations that restrict the direct claim to controlled operational training augmentation.
